source_paper: Experts’ recommendations for the management of adult patients with cardiogenic shock
author: Nadia Aissaoui et al.
pearl: For patients with cardiogenic shock, utilize a multidisciplinary 'Shock Team' consisting of at least a heart failure specialist, an interventional cardiologist, and an intensivist or cardiac anesthesiologist to facilitate rapid SCAI staging and collaborative decision-making regarding temporary mechanical circulatory support (t-MCS).

source_paper: JCS 2026 Guideline on the Management of Infective Endocarditis
author: Chisato Izumi et al.
pearl: Staphylococcus aureus (especially MRSA) has surpassed viridans group streptococci as the most common and virulent pathogen in developed countries, causing rapid valvular destruction, perivalvular abscesses, and systemic embolization.

source_paper: JCS 2026 Guideline on the Management of Infective Endocarditis
author: Chisato Izumi et al.
pearl: Under the 2023 Duke-ISCVID criteria, a definitive clinical diagnosis of infective endocarditis requires the presence of either 2 major criteria, 1 major and 3 minor criteria, or 5 minor criteria.

source_paper: JCS 2026 Guideline on the Management of Infective Endocarditis
author: Chisato Izumi et al.
pearl: To satisfy the major microbiological criterion, typical pathogens (such as S. aureus, S. lugdunensis, E. faecalis, VGS, and HACEK) must be isolated from at least 2 separate blood culture sets, whereas less common pathogens require isolation from at least 3 separate sets.

source_paper: JCS 2026 Guideline on the Management of Infective Endocarditis
author: Chisato Izumi et al.
pearl: Major microbiological criteria for intracellular pathogens are met with a positive PCR for Coxiella burnetii, Bartonella spp., or Tropheryma whipplei, an IgG anti-phase I titer for C. burnetii > 1:800, or an IFA IgG titer >= 1:800 for B. henselae or B. quintana.

source_paper: JCS 2026 Guideline on the Management of Infective Endocarditis
author: Chisato Izumi et al.
pearl: When using 18F-FDG PET/CT to identify abnormal metabolic activity as a major imaging criterion, the prosthetic valve must have been implanted for at least 3 months to avoid false-positive results from physiological postoperative inflammation.

source_paper: JCS 2026 Guideline on the Management of Infective Endocarditis
author: Chisato Izumi et al.
pearl: Glomerulonephritis qualifies as an immunological minor criterion for infective endocarditis and is clinically defined by acute kidney injury associated with hematuria, proteinuria, red cell casts, or hypocomplementemia.

source_paper: Capillary refill time changes are associated with Vascular Waterfall response in post-cardiac surgery patients
author: Stefan Andrei, Stéphane Bar, Maxime Nguyen, Bélaid Bouhemad, Dan Longrois, Pierre-Grégoire Guinot
pearl: For septic shock, initial management requires prompt administration of broad-spectrum antibiotics and fluid resuscitation within the first hour.

source_paper: Capillary refill time changes are associated with Vascular Waterfall response in post-cardiac surgery patients
author: Stefan Andrei, Stéphane Bar, Maxime Nguyen, Bélaid Bouhemad, Dan Longrois, Pierre-Grégoire Guinot
pearl: Vasopressors should be initiated early in septic shock; norepinephrine is the preferred agent, with a target Mean Arterial Pressure (MAP) of 65 mmHg.

source_paper: Capillary refill time changes are associated with Vascular Waterfall response in post-cardiac surgery patients
author: Stefan Andrei, Stéphane Bar, Maxime Nguyen, Bélaid Bouhemad, Dan Longrois, Pierre-Grégoire Guinot
pearl: If MAP remains below 65 mmHg despite initial fluid resuscitation and norepinephrine, consider adding vasopressin or epinephrine as second-line agents.

source_paper: Capillary refill time changes are associated with Vascular Waterfall response in post-cardiac surgery patients
author: Stefan Andrei, Stéphane Bar, Maxime Nguyen, Bélaid Bouhemad, Dan Longrois, Pierre-Grégoire Guinot
pearl: A lactate level >2 mmol/L suggests tissue hypoperfusion and requires aggressive management (e.g., fluids, vasopressors).

source_paper: Capillary refill time changes are associated with Vascular Waterfall response in post-cardiac surgery patients
author: Stefan Andrei, Stéphane Bar, Maxime Nguyen, Bélaid Bouhemad, Dan Longrois, Pierre-Grégoire Guinot
pearl: Source control is paramount in septic shock; identify and manage all potential foci of infection (e.g., abscesses, infected lines).

source_paper: Capillary refill time changes are associated with Vascular Waterfall response in post-cardiac surgery patients
author: Stefan Andrei, Stéphane Bar, Maxime Nguyen, Bélaid Bouhemad, Dan Longrois, Pierre-Grégoire Guinot
pearl: In patients with acute respiratory failure requiring mechanical ventilation, PEEP titration should be individualized based on oxygenation goals and hemodynamic stability.

source_paper: Capillary refill time changes are associated with Vascular Waterfall response in post-cardiac surgery patients
author: Stefan Andrei, Stéphane Bar, Maxime Nguyen, Bélaid Bouhemad, Dan Longrois, Pierre-Grégoire Guinot
pearl: Consider prone positioning for severe Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome (ARDS) if the patient is hemodynamically stable and has refractory hypoxemia despite optimal ventilator settings.

source_paper: Capillary refill time changes are associated with Vascular Waterfall response in post-cardiac surgery patients
author: Stefan Andrei, Stéphane Bar, Maxime Nguyen, Bélaid Bouhemad, Dan Longrois, Pierre-Grégoire Guinot
pearl: Daily reassessment of sedation needs and weaning readiness is crucial to minimize ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP) risk.

source_paper: Capillary refill time changes are associated with Vascular Waterfall response in post-cardiac surgery patients
author: Stefan Andrei, Stéphane Bar, Maxime Nguyen, Bélaid Bouhemad, Dan Longrois, Pierre-Grégoire Guinot
pearl: Early mobilization protocols should be initiated as soon as medically stable to prevent muscle atrophy and improve pulmonary function in critically ill patients.

source_paper: Capillary refill time changes are associated with Vascular Waterfall response in post-cardiac surgery patients
author: Stefan Andrei, Stéphane Bar, Maxime Nguyen, Bélaid Bouhemad, Dan Longrois, Pierre-Grégoire Guinot
pearl: Targeting a SpO2 of 90-96% is generally appropriate for ventilated ARDS patients, avoiding excessive oxygen delivery that can cause oxygen toxicity.

source_paper: Capillary refill time changes are associated with Vascular Waterfall response in post-cardiac surgery patients
author: Stefan Andrei, Stéphane Bar, Maxime Nguyen, Bélaid Bouhemad, Dan Longrois, Pierre-Grégoire Guinot
pearl: Continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT) may be preferred over intermittent hemodialysis in critically ill patients with acute kidney injury due to better hemodynamic stability and lower bleeding risk.

source_paper: Diagnostic and Prognostic Value of the Venous Excess Ultrasound Grading System: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
author: Peter Klompmaker et al.
pearl: VExUS is an ultrasound protocol integrating IVC diameter (≥2 cm) and Doppler flow patterns of hepatic, portal, and intrarenal veins to assess fluid tolerance.

source_paper: Diagnostic and Prognostic Value of the Venous Excess Ultrasound Grading System: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
author: Peter Klompmaker et al.
pearl: In cardiac patients, VExUS grade 2 or higher significantly predicts AKI (pooled OR 4.44) and mortality (pooled OR 3.17).

source_paper: Diagnostic and Prognostic Value of the Venous Excess Ultrasound Grading System: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
author: Peter Klompmaker et al.
pearl: For general critically ill patients, only VExUS grade 3 is significantly associated with increased risk of all-cause mortality (OR 2.42).

source_paper: Diagnostic and Prognostic Value of the Venous Excess Ultrasound Grading System: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
author: Peter Klompmaker et al.
pearl: VExUS should be interpreted as a marker of 'reduced fluid tolerance' rather than solely volume overload.

source_paper: Diagnostic and Prognostic Value of the Venous Excess Ultrasound Grading System: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
author: Peter Klompmaker et al.
pearl: When VExUS is grade 2 or 3, clinicians must carefully consider the risks associated with further fluid administration.

source_paper: Diagnostic and Prognostic Value of the Venous Excess Ultrasound Grading System: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
author: Peter Klompmaker et al.
pearl: VExUS grades are useful for detecting elevated RAP (>10 mm Hg), showing a sensitivity of 87% and specificity of 90% when grade 2 or higher is present.

source_paper: Central Venous Pressure Revisited: Physiology, Pitfalls, Misconceptions, and Modern Clinical Interpretation in Critical Care
author: Cesare Biuzzi et al.
pearl: Never administer fluids solely to 'normalize' a low CVP; instead, monitor the change in CVP during a bolus: if it rises by > 3 mmHg without increased cardiac output/stroke volume, stop infusion immediately.

source_paper: Central Venous Pressure Revisited: Physiology, Pitfalls, Misconceptions, and Modern Clinical Interpretation in Critical Care
author: Cesare Biuzzi et al.
pearl: In septic shock or cardiorenal syndrome, target maintaining renal perfusion gradient (MAP - CVP) rather than normalizing CVP to maximize kidney function and avoid positive fluid balances.

source_paper: Central Venous Pressure Revisited: Physiology, Pitfalls, Misconceptions, and Modern Clinical Interpretation in Critical Care
author: Cesare Biuzzi et al.
pearl: Integrate CVP monitoring with bedside echocardiography and VExUS Doppler grading to create a comprehensive 'congestion profile' before initiating any fluid removal or aggressive resuscitation.

source_paper: Central Venous Pressure Revisited: Physiology, Pitfalls, Misconceptions, and Modern Clinical Interpretation in Critical Care
author: Cesare Biuzzi et al.
pearl: CVP must be measured at end-expiration (or zeroed accordingly) to minimize the overestimation of preload caused by mechanical ventilation and PEEP.

source_paper: Central Venous Pressure Revisited: Physiology, Pitfalls, Misconceptions, and Modern Clinical Interpretation in Critical Care
author: Cesare Biuzzi et al.
pearl: The Mean Perfusion Pressure (MPP = MAP - CVP) is a critical determinant of organ perfusion; elevated CVP directly narrows this gradient, impairing renal function even if MAP is maintained.

source_paper: Central Venous Pressure Revisited: Physiology, Pitfalls, Misconceptions, and Modern Clinical Interpretation in Critical Care
author: Cesare Biuzzi et al.
pearl: A rapid, disproportionate rise in CVP (e.g., delta CVP > 2-3 mmHg) during a fluid challenge without increased stroke volume indicates limited fluid tolerance and requires immediate cessation of fluids.

source_paper: Central Venous Pressure Revisited: Physiology, Pitfalls, Misconceptions, and Modern Clinical Interpretation in Critical Care
author: Cesare Biuzzi et al.
pearl: The historical goal-directed target for static CVP (8-12 mmHg or 12-15 mmHg in mechanically ventilated patients) has been abandoned by major guidelines due to lack of survival benefit in septic shock.

source_paper: The Intra-aortic Balloon Pump: A Focused Review of Physiology, Transport Logistics, Mechanics, and Complications
author: Lauren E. Gillespie, MD et al.
pearl: Mandatory neurovascular checks must be performed pre- and post-transport for IABP patients to monitor for limb ischemia and radial pulse changes.

source_paper: The Intra-aortic Balloon Pump: A Focused Review of Physiology, Transport Logistics, Mechanics, and Complications
author: Lauren E. Gillespie, MD et al.
pearl: For IABP timing errors (early/late inflation or deflation), 'Automode' should be used for correction rather than manual adjustment.

source_paper: The Intra-aortic Balloon Pump: A Focused Review of Physiology, Transport Logistics, Mechanics, and Complications
author: Lauren E. Gillespie, MD et al.
pearl: During CPR, continue IABP support and switch to pressure trigger mode if the ECG signal is unreliable due to artifact.

source_paper: The Intra-aortic Balloon Pump: A Focused Review of Physiology, Transport Logistics, Mechanics, and Complications
author: Lauren E. Gillespie, MD et al.
pearl: A 'Rapid Gas Loss' alarm indicates possible balloon rupture; immediately stop the pump and prepare for catheter removal.

source_paper: The Intra-aortic Balloon Pump: A Focused Review of Physiology, Transport Logistics, Mechanics, and Complications
author: Lauren E. Gillespie, MD et al.
pearl: The IABP battery life is typically 2-2.25 hours, necessitating that backup power sources are always available during transport.

source_paper: The Intra-aortic Balloon Pump: A Focused Review of Physiology, Transport Logistics, Mechanics, and Complications
author: Lauren E. Gillespie, MD et al.
pearl: Maintain the head of bed at less than 30 degrees (<30°) during IABP transport to prevent femoral catheter kinking or bleeding.

source_paper: The Intra-aortic Balloon Pump: A Focused Review of Physiology, Transport Logistics, Mechanics, and Complications
author: Lauren E. Gillespie, MD et al.
pearl: The device functions via counterpulsation: inflating in diastole (T-P interval) to increase coronary perfusion pressure and deflating in systole (QRS-T interval) to reduce left ventricular afterload.

source_paper: Bridging strategies for heart-lung transplantation: Current concepts and mechanical support modalities
author: K. Hoetzenecker, J.W. Stokes, M. Bacchetta, and O. Mercier
pearl: Prioritize 'sports-mode' ECMO for stable HLTx candidates needing longer bridge times to maintain ambulation, utilizing surgical graft implantation into the subclavian artery and internal jugular vein.

source_paper: Bridging strategies for heart-lung transplantation: Current concepts and mechanical support modalities
author: K. Hoetzenecker, J.W. Stokes, M. Bacchetta, and O. Mercier
pearl: When using VA-ECMO in patients with LV failure, monitor for LV distention and pulmonary congestion; decompression methods include atrial septostomy, IABP, Impella, or direct LV venting.

source_paper: Bridging strategies for heart-lung transplantation: Current concepts and mechanical support modalities
author: K. Hoetzenecker, J.W. Stokes, M. Bacchetta, and O. Mercier
pearl: In Eisenmenger patients, assess bleeding risk due to extensive collateralization before initiating ECMO.

source_paper: Bridging strategies for heart-lung transplantation: Current concepts and mechanical support modalities
author: K. Hoetzenecker, J.W. Stokes, M. Bacchetta, and O. Mercier
pearl: Multidisciplinary team involvement is mandatory for selecting the appropriate ECMO configuration based on patient pathophysiology (e.g., LV/RV failure, shunt dynamics).

source_paper: Bridging strategies for heart-lung transplantation: Current concepts and mechanical support modalities
author: K. Hoetzenecker, J.W. Stokes, M. Bacchetta, and O. Mercier
pearl: For Eisenmenger patients with an ASD, a dual-lumen cannula can be positioned to direct oxygenated blood across the defect to improve systemic oxygenation.

source_paper: Bridging strategies for heart-lung transplantation: Current concepts and mechanical support modalities
author: K. Hoetzenecker, J.W. Stokes, M. Bacchetta, and O. Mercier
pearl: The V-PA ECMO (Oxy-RVAD) is currently not recommended as a standard bridging protocol for HLTx due to limited data.

source_paper: Echocardiography before non-cardiac surgery: current knowledge, guideline recommendations, and clinical evidence – a narrative review
author: Viktoria Mertin, Alexandra Stroda, Tobias Bruns, Giovanna Lurati Buse
pearl: Avoid routine preoperative TTE in asymptomatic, stable patients due to lack of evidence for improved clinical outcomes.

source_paper: Echocardiography before non-cardiac surgery: current knowledge, guideline recommendations, and clinical evidence – a narrative review
author: Viktoria Mertin, Alexandra Stroda, Tobias Bruns, Giovanna Lurati Buse
pearl: Utilize Focused Cardiac Ultrasound (FOCUS) as a targeted tool for specific clinical questions (e.g., suspected heart failure or valvular disease) rather than broad screening.

source_paper: Echocardiography before non-cardiac surgery: current knowledge, guideline recommendations, and clinical evidence – a narrative review
author: Viktoria Mertin, Alexandra Stroda, Tobias Bruns, Giovanna Lurati Buse
pearl: Prioritize clinical assessment (symptoms, functional capacity, biomarkers like NT-proBNP) over indiscriminate imaging when assessing patients for non-cardiac surgery.

source_paper: Echocardiography before non-cardiac surgery: current knowledge, guideline recommendations, and clinical evidence – a narrative review
author: Viktoria Mertin, Alexandra Stroda, Tobias Bruns, Giovanna Lurati Buse
pearl: The ESC 2022 guidelines recommend preoperative echocardiography Class I for symptomatic patients, genetic cardiomyopathy, or high-risk non-cardiac surgery with poor functional capacity/high NT-proBNP.

source_paper: Echocardiography before non-cardiac surgery: current knowledge, guideline recommendations, and clinical evidence – a narrative review
author: Viktoria Mertin, Alexandra Stroda, Tobias Bruns, Giovanna Lurati Buse
pearl: The ESC 2022 guidelines suggest FOCUS (Class IIb) for intermediate-risk non-cardiac surgery if the patient has poor functional capacity or high NT-proBNP to avoid surgical delay.

source_paper: Echocardiography before non-cardiac surgery: current knowledge, guideline recommendations, and clinical evidence – a narrative review
author: Viktoria Mertin, Alexandra Stroda, Tobias Bruns, Giovanna Lurati Buse
pearl: The AHA/ACC 2024 guidelines recommend preoperative echocardiography Class I for patients with new dyspnea, physical exam findings of heart failure, or suspected severe valvular disease.

source_paper: Echocardiography before non-cardiac surgery: current knowledge, guideline recommendations, and clinical evidence – a narrative review
author: Viktoria Mertin, Alexandra Stroda, Tobias Bruns, Giovanna Lurati Buse
pearl: The AHA/ACC 2024 guidelines suggest FOCUS (Class IIb) for known LV dysfunction (stable) or previously documented LV dysfunction without TTE in >12 months.

source_paper: The gut–heart axis in sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy: pathophysiology, microbial metabolites, and cardiovascular insights
author: Zhaokai Li and Guoqiang Zhang
pearl: Interventions for sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy (SICM) should target the first 24-48 hours of septic shock to stabilize the gut barrier.

source_paper: The gut–heart axis in sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy: pathophysiology, microbial metabolites, and cardiovascular insights
author: Zhaokai Li and Guoqiang Zhang
pearl: Early enteral nutrition with fiber is prioritized in sepsis to support beneficial short-chain fatty acid (SCFA) production.

source_paper: The gut–heart axis in sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy: pathophysiology, microbial metabolites, and cardiovascular insights
author: Zhaokai Li and Guoqiang Zhang
pearl: Clinical trials for SICM should adopt phenotype-enriched designs and utilize cardiac-specific endpoints such as LVEF recovery or vasopressor-free days.

source_paper: The gut–heart axis in sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy: pathophysiology, microbial metabolites, and cardiovascular insights
author: Zhaokai Li and Guoqiang Zhang
pearl: Avoid indiscriminate use of probiotics in hemodynamically unstable patients due to potential risks of microbial translocation.

source_paper: The gut–heart axis in sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy: pathophysiology, microbial metabolites, and cardiovascular insights
author: Zhaokai Li and Guoqiang Zhang
pearl: Butyrate is a protective SCFA that activates GPR43/GPR41/GPR109A and reduces cytokine storms, suggesting cardioprotective potential in sepsis.

source_paper: The gut–heart axis in sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy: pathophysiology, microbial metabolites, and cardiovascular insights
author: Zhaokai Li and Guoqiang Zhang
pearl: Indole-3-propionic acid (IPA) protects against cardiomyocyte injury by activating AhR and inhibiting NF-kB/NLRP3 pathways.

source_paper: The gut–heart axis in sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy: pathophysiology, microbial metabolites, and cardiovascular insights
author: Zhaokai Li and Guoqiang Zhang
pearl: Activation of FXR and TGR5 using bile acids (e.g., Obeticholic acid or UDCA) shows promise in preserving mitochondrial integrity and reducing reactive oxygen species (ROS).

source_paper: The Effect of Restrictive vs Liberal Blood Transfusion Strategy on Subsequent Myocardial Infarction Type
author: Andrew P. DeFilippis et al.
pearl: In patients with MI and anemia, the choice between a restrictive or liberal blood transfusion strategy does not differentially affect the risk of subsequent Myocardial Infarction (MI), regardless of whether it is Type 1 or Type 2.

source_paper: Magnesium Sulfate to Prevent Perioperative Atrial Fibrillation in Cardiac Surgery: A Randomized Clinical Trial
author: Manon Meerman, MD et al.
pearl: Routine prophylactic IV magnesium sulfate targeting 1.5-2.0 mmol/L does not prevent postoperative atrial fibrillation (POAF) in cardiac surgery patients and may increase vasopressor requirements.

source_paper: Magnesium Sulfate to Prevent Perioperative Atrial Fibrillation in Cardiac Surgery: A Randomized Clinical Trial
author: Manon Meerman, MD et al.
pearl: Aggressive magnesium supplementation may cause vasodilation, potentially increasing the requirement for vasopressors like noradrenaline in the early postoperative period (40.2% vs 28.6%; p=0.05).

source_paper: Magnesium Sulfate to Prevent Perioperative Atrial Fibrillation in Cardiac Surgery: A Randomized Clinical Trial
author: Manon Meerman, MD et al.
pearl: The primary focus of electrolyte management should be correcting hypomagnesemia (< 1.0 mmol/L) rather than aiming for prophylactic hypermagnesemia.
